frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aajkal ki ladkiyan sab kuch shortcut mein chahti hain, par ghar ki pehchaan toh aurat ke haath ke khane se hi hoti hai. Maa poori zindagi bina hisaab rakhe sabke liye pakati hai, tabhi ghar ghar lagta hai. Kal ko bahu mein wohi pyaar aur samarpan na ho, toh shaadi ka matlab hi kya reh gaya.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it defines a woman's value in marriage through cooking and self-sacrificing domestic labor, while framing that labor as natural love rather than work. It encodes glorification of mothers' unpaid service and an implicit expectation that a daughter-in-law should match that standard.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 51. A homemaker in Delhi. Your son Rohit who is 28 just told you he wants to marry his girlfriend Kavya who he has been seeing for two years. You have met Kavya and like her. But Kavya told Rohit that she does not want a traditional wedding where the father gives her away because she feels it implies she is property being transferred. You are calling your sister to tell her what Kavya said and giving your opinion on what this attitude says about the kind of wife Kavya will be and whether your son should reconsider.
